# Category: cloud_monitoring

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-01'  # AWS version, update as needed for template updates to maintain backward compatibility (http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/)  
Transform: aws_dynamic_ec2_instance_type if not provided in S3 and by default will use t3 instance type, based on region requirements  # for auto-scaling support (https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/transform-auto-scaling-with-elb-loadbalancer)
Resources:
  MyVPC10GbeSubnet256GBNetworkInterfaceSecurityGroupEIPAllowsSSH: #EC2 instance resource (use a unique name for each one, it's required by AWS CloudFormation but not in this template )  
    Type: 'AWS::EC2::VPC'  # define your VPC resources here. It includes Subnets & SecurityGroups as well..You can also add internet gateway and route tables if you want to connect the instance directly with an Internet or a VPN Connection (if required). AWS automatically allocates Public IPs from their pool for us
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: 10.254.36.0/27   #You can adjust this value based on your requirements, and then use it in Subnets where necessary (example if you have to run multiple instances of a EC2 instance).Also define subnet tags which would allow for easy tracking & management 
      EnableDnsSupport: True    # enable DNS resolution support     You can also add more VPC attributes like this, based on your requirements. If needed then include those below the comment above line (additional properties goes here)  
       Tags:                 
         - Key: Name 
           Value: MyVPC10GbeSubnet256GBNetworkInterfaceSecurityGroupEIPAllowsSSH #This is a unique name for your Vpc resource    You can change this to reflect the purpose of each AWS Resource.   If you are not sure about these, then don't use it (if possible remove all lines)
         - Key: Description #You might want some way to provide more information on how CloudFormation uses your resources in general    For instance : The CidrBlock is the IPv4 range for a VPC. A subnet can be used with any number of instances (EC2 Instances). This will help you understand this template better
  MyVPC10GbeSubnet256GBNetworkInterfaceSecurityGroupEIPAllowsSSHInstance:   #define EC2 instance resource here    It includes all required properties for an AWS::EC2::Instace type (like its Vpc and Subnets, Security Groups etc)
     Type : 'AWS::EC2::Instance'      Properties:  //your instances should include more specific details as per your needs e.g network interface mappings & subnet assignments based on the instance’s availability zone or any other rules (https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide//aws-resource-ec2-instance.html)
     DependsOn: MyVPC10GbeSubnet256GBNetworkInterfaceSecurityGroupEIPAllowsSSH      #This line will ensure the EC2 Instance is available only after VPC has been created (you might want to set it up in another step, this one depends on above)
  //... //... Add other resources as per requirement ... //    Note: This Stack will not create an S3 Bucket, you have to use AWS CloudFormation for that also.. (this is a very long process in itself). You need at least one IAM role and policy definition which your EC2 instances can assume if needed. 
//... Add other resources as per requirement ... //   This Stack will not create an RDS Database, you have to use AWS CloudFormation for that also.. (this is a very long process in itself). You need at least one IAM role and policy definition which your EC2 instances can assume if needed.
